completion_summary: Implemented a deterministic offline 60/120/240-step refinement audit for the existing cooling-crystallization PBM, including component material closure, step target/impurity transfer closure, no-breakage particle-count closure, explicit non-convergence diagnostics, model card, provider, and hash-bound cool_crystallize vNext adapter. Validated by adapter intake 4/4 accepted, 763 passed/11 skipped full suite, 48 passed after merging current main, Ruff, mypy, claim audit, and 50 deterministic audits (~24.3 ms/audit; report sha256 67a575de7452cb6b50b2241042cb3c086bc66b537f53b42aa428f3dd056c5846). The finest-grid recovery delta is 1.491% under a 2% threshold. Reference boundary pinned to IDAES eed7cebc3d99be616ee7ead203cecaee9f81ac01 idaes/core/base/control_volume0d.py for material-balance/state conventions only, not crystallization kinetics. Limitations: offline diagnostic only; it does not validate PBM parameters, experimental CSD, energy coupling, polymorphs, agglomeration, or breakage, and does not change v0.3/runtime maturity. Implementation commit 89a1e78f03a6b0a3e3d161fc99b9f2fcd0d31a60; integrated main 3bcdb666eeb47ec43ad50e1aaf360f178a7e7f15.
notes: No runtime, task, v0.3, existing crystallization model, or WF-110 path changes. IDAES is used only as a material-balance/state-variable convention boundary, not as a crystallization kinetics backend.
scope: Deterministic time-grid convergence and material/step-ledger closure audit for the existing cooling-crystallization PBM, with a vNext diagnostic adapter
